user_intent_preservation.functional_behavior: Generate 3-phase PWM on GTM.TOM1 using pins P00.2–P00.7, center-aligned at 20 kHz. Initialize duties U/V/W to 25/50/75% and every 500 ms increment each by +10% with wrap in 0–100% (clamp 100% to period-1 tick). Dead time 0.5 µs is omitted now (no complementary HS/LS); if complementary outputs are later required, enable TOM1 CDTM/DTM and apply 0.5 µs dead time.
user_intent_preservation.technical_approach: Use the unified high-level IfxGtm_Pwm driver on TC38x to control TOM1 channels 5–10 mapped to P00.2–P00.7 with IfxGtm_PinMap_TC38x_LFBGA516. Configure GTM CMU FXCLK (~100 MHz), center-aligned PWM at 20 kHz, and start synced channels. Update duties periodically via IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDutyImmediate with a 500 ms schedule (e.g., IfxStm_Timer). Keep no dead time now; if later needed, configure TOM1 CDTM/DTM and use IfxGtm_Pwm_updateChannelsDeadTimeImmediate.

driver_selection.selection_rationale: Unified high-level PWM fits advanced multi-channel synchronized PWM on GTM.TOM1, supports center-aligned mode, complementary pairing and dead time if later required. It is available in the template for TC3xx.

runtime_function.name: GTM_TOM_3_Phase_Inverter_PWM_stepDuties
runtime_function.purpose: Every 500 ms, increment U/V/W duties by +10% with wrap in 0–100%. When request equals 100%, clamp to period-1 tick by slightly reducing the duty request to avoid a full-period compare match.

migration_notes.context_preserved: TOM1 and pin map P00.2–P00.7 are kept. Center-aligned 20 kHz, initial U/V/W 25/50/75%, and 500 ms stepped duty updates are implemented with synchronized updates. Dead time is omitted now as complementary outputs are not required; If later enabled, TOM1 CDTM/DTM can be configured while staying on IfxGtm_Pwm using dead-time update APIs.
